5.title: Crop Classification Methods
5.findings: Crop-type classification from satellite image time series evolved from traditional machine learning (Random Forest, SVM) trained on hand-crafted features (vegetation indices, texture) - interpretable but dependent on manual feature design and brittle when crop mixtures or temporal patterns change - to deep learning with end-to-end representation learning that now dominates. Reported accuracies vary by context (e.g. ~77-80% several weeks before harvest with RF/SVM and active learning). In-season classification, growth-stage normalisation and active learning reduce labelling needs.

refs.t: Spatially Explicit Active Learning for Crop-Type Mapping from SITS
Resilient In-Season Crop Type Classification (Growth Stage Normalization)
Deep Learning for Satellite Image Time Series Analysis: A Review (IEEE GRSM 2024)

6.title: Building a Workflow
6.findings: Reproducible, scalable EO workflows are increasingly expressed as technology-agnostic openEO 'process graphs' on cloud back-ends (Copernicus Data Space Ecosystem), reducing code complexity and enabling open-science reproducibility. A canonical crop-mapping workflow composites Sentinel-2 L2A (10-daily medians) and Sentinel-1 sigma0 backscatter, masks agriculture (e.g. WorldCover), aligns to a common grid and fuses into a single datacube. ESA WorldCereal delivers a global, seasonal, reproducible crop and irrigation mapping system on this stack.

refs.t: Continental-scale crop type mapping in openEO platform
Copernicus Data Space Ecosystem: public cloud processing for EO (Scientific Data 2026)
Open-EO/openeo-gfmap: generic framework for EO mapping
Deploying Geospatial Foundation Models: Lessons from WorldCereal

7.title: Crop and Yield Basics
7.findings: Crop yield estimation combines two families: process-based crop-growth model simulation and remote-sensing observation, fused through data assimilation - the vital tool for regional yield forecasting. RS supplies cultivation area, phenology and growth status (e.g. leaf area index from Sentinel-2) that supplement/parameterise crop models. Common designs assimilate LAI into WOFOST/SAFY via the Ensemble Kalman Filter (EnKF), and hybrid EnKF-RF frameworks couple assimilation with machine learning for early-season, large-area forecasting.

refs.t: Crop yield estimation by assimilation of crop models and RS: a systematic evaluation (Agric. Systems 2023)
RS-crop-model assimilation + seasonal forecasts for early-season yield forecasting (AgForMet 2021)
Wheat growth monitoring and yield via RS assimilation into SAFY

8.title: Accuracy Assessment
8.findings: Rigorous map accuracy assessment rests on three components: sampling design, response (reference) design and analysis. The confusion/error matrix is the starting point, and good practice is to report area estimated from the reference classification with confidence intervals. Probability sampling and design-based inference are essential. Documented BAD practices to avoid: using estimation formulas that do not match the sampling design, normalising the error matrix, and relying on the kappa coefficient. Key decisions: stratified vs simple random, clusters vs not, systematic vs random.

refs.t: Stehman & Foody, Key issues in rigorous accuracy assessment of land cover products (RSE 2019)
Olofsson et al., Good practices for estimating area and assessing accuracy of land change (RSE 2014)
CEOS WGCV LPV Land Cover & Change accuracy protocol (2025)

10.title: Model Training and Algorithms
10.findings: For satellite image time-series crop classification, Random Forest remains a strong, interpretable baseline; deep architectures generally improve accuracy: 1D temporal CNNs (TempCNN) use multi-scale temporal filters and better delineate spectrally similar crops than RF; RNN/LSTM (incl. attention) model sequence dynamics; and Transformer (self-attention, e.g. PSE+TAE) architectures model temporal (and spatial) dependencies with strong results. Benchmarks such as BreizhCrops standardise comparison; interpretability of multi-temporal deep models is an active concern.

refs.t: Investigating Temporal CNNs for SITS Classification: a survey (TempCNN)
BreizhCrops: A Time Series Dataset for Crop Type Mapping
Towards interpreting multi-temporal deep learning models in crop mapping (RSE 2021)
